quick_skim.title: Feature Alignment Determines Fusion Strategy: A Comparative Study of Cross-Attention and Concatenation in Multimodal Learning
quick_skim.abstract: The choice between cross-attention and concatenation for multimodal fusion remains governed by practitioner intuition rather than principled understanding. In this paper, we demonstrate that feature alignment quality, not data scale alone, is the primary determinant of which fusion strategy excels. Through controlled experiments on Flickr8k using two feature extraction backbones (ResNet18 and CLIP ViT-B/32), we show that concatenation outperforms cross-attention by 4.1-5.1 percentage points across all tested scales (2048-16384 samples) when features are pre-aligned by a vision-language pretraining objective. We provide a theoretical explanation grounded in sample complexity analysis: concatenation requires O(d_v + d_t) samples to learn its fusion projection, while cross-attention requires O(d_v * d_t) samples to learn bilinear attention weights, over 256 times as many for 512-dimensional CLIP features. When features are already aligned, the approximation error gap between the two methods vanishes, and concatenation's sample efficiency dominates at all practical dataset sizes. An alignment degradation study confirms a monotonic trend: as feature alignment degrades, concatenation's advantage grows from 1.3% to 2.8%. These findings provide a principled decision framework for fusion method selection in multimodal systems, with direct implications for the design of Multimodal Large Language Models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: comparative study of cross-attention and concatenation fusion strategies in multimodal learning, providing transferable insights for multimodal remote sensing fusion
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态学习中跨注意力和拼接融合策略的比较研究，为多模态遥感融合提供了可迁移的方法论见解
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多模态学习中跨注意力和拼接融合策略的比较研究，为多模态遥感融合提供了可迁移的方法论见解
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多模态学习中跨注意力和拼接融合策略的比较研究，为多模态遥感融合提供了可迁移的方法论见解
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper systematically compares cross-attention and concatenation for multimodal fusion, finding that feature alignment quality determines the optimal strategy; concatenation outperforms cross-attention by 4-5% when features are pre-aligned, with implications for any multimodal fusion task.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态融合中跨注意力与拼接的选择一直缺乏原则性指导。本文通过可控实验证明，特征对齐质量而非数据规模是决定哪种融合策略更优的主要因素：当特征经过视觉-语言预训练对齐后，拼接在各类尺度下均比跨注意力高出4.1-5.1个百分点。研究从样本复杂度角度给出理论解释，为多模态融合设计提供了通用准则，对遥感等多模态任务有直接借鉴意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多模态融合中跨注意力与拼接的选择一直缺乏原则性指导。本文通过可控实验证明，特征对齐质量而非数据规模是决定哪种融合策略更优的主要因素：当特征经过视觉-语言预训练对齐后，拼接在各类尺度下均比跨注意力高出4.1-5.1个百分点。研究从样本复杂度角度给出理论解释，为多模态融合设计提供了通用准则，对遥感等多模态任务有直接借鉴意义。

quick_skim.title: Disentangled Fine-Grained Prototype Learning for Incomplete Image-Tabular Classification
quick_skim.abstract: The missing-modality problem poses a significant challenge in image-tabular multimodal learning across a wide range of multimedia applications, including product understanding, recommendation systems, and medical diagnosis. This challenge is particularly pronounced when the two modalities are highly heterogeneous, as images and tabular attributes differ substantially in their semantic granularity and data distributions. Existing methods learn modality-invariant representations through disentanglement and alignment over global token-averaged features, capturing only coarse cross-modal consistency and overlooking fine-grained semantic and distributional misalignment, which hampers the exploitation of complementary cues under missing modalities. To address this, we propose DFPL, a novel framework for fine-grained prototype learning. Specifically, Shared-Specific Prototype Modeling (SSPM) extracts compact and diverse shared and modality-specific prototypes, and further performs prototype-level disentanglement to suppress redundant intra-modality correlations. Additionally, we propose a Prototype-guided Fine-grained Alignment (PFA) module that jointly enforces prototype-level distribution matching and prototype-to-class semantic alignment within a unified prototype space, thereby preserving both fine-grained distributional and semantic consistency across modalities. We further introduce a Class-aware Multi-scale Aggregation (CMA) module to adaptively aggregate shared semantics and modality-specific characteristics from global and prototype levels for robust predictions. Extensive experiments on three diverse image-tabular benchmarks demonstrate the superiority of our method compared to the previous approaches under various missing-modality settings. Code will be made publicly available.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: addresses missing-modality problem in multimodal learning with image and tabular data
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 处理图像与表格数据中的缺失模态问题
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 处理图像与表格数据中的缺失模态问题
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 处理图像与表格数据中的缺失模态问题
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes disentangled fine-grained prototype learning to handle missing modalities in image-tabular classification by leveraging fine-grained cross-modal alignment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对图像-表格多模态学习中的缺失模态挑战，本文提出解缠细粒度原型学习方法。该方法通过细粒度跨模态对齐，超越传统的全局特征平均，有效利用互补信息。在多个基准数据集上，方法在缺失模态场景下显著提升了分类性能，为不完整多模态学习提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对图像-表格多模态学习中的缺失模态挑战，本文提出解缠细粒度原型学习方法。该方法通过细粒度跨模态对齐，超越传统的全局特征平均，有效利用互补信息。在多个基准数据集上，方法在缺失模态场景下显著提升了分类性能，为不完整多模态学习提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: GMBFormer: An NDVI-Guided Global Memory Bank Transformer for Urban Green-Space Extraction from Ultra-High-Resolution Imagery
quick_skim.abstract: Urban green-space extraction from ultra-high-resolution (UHR) imagery is commonly performed patch by patch, which limits semantic reuse among spatially separated but visually similar vegetation patterns. Directly injecting the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) into red-green-blue (RGB) backbones can also blur the roles of visual appearance learning and physical vegetation confidence. We propose GMBFormer, a SegFormer-based framework that replaces adjacency-driven feature propagation with selective, similarity-driven prototype retrieval. Only RGB channels enter the backbone and decoder, while NDVI is decoupled as a physics-informed gate that admits high-confidence vegetation descriptors into a compact global memory bank through momentum updates. During training and inference, the current patch queries stored prototypes through memory-mediated cross-attention, and the retrieved response is integrated with bounded overhead. Experiments use a self-constructed Chengdu UHR dataset with 7,700 labeled 512 x 512 patches and two reduced-label settings derived from the public International Society for Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing (ISPRS) Potsdam dataset. Under the same training and evaluation protocol, GMBFormer obtains mean intersection over union (mIoU)/mean Dice (mDice) scores of 89.25%/94.31%, 92.17%/95.92%, and 83.72%/90.86%, respectively, improving the controlled SegFormer-B4 baseline in each setting. Ablation studies indicate that decoupled NDVI admission, memory retrieval, capacity, and momentum jointly shape the final performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: fuses RGB and NDVI for urban green-space extraction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 融合RGB和NDVI进行城市绿地提取
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 融合RGB和NDVI进行城市绿地提取
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 融合RGB和NDVI进行城市绿地提取
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GMBFormer uses an NDVI-guided global memory bank to fuse RGB and NDVI for urban green-space extraction from UHR imagery.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对超分辨率遥感影像城市绿地提取中语义复用不足及NDVI与RGB特征混淆的问题，提出GMBFormer框架，将NDVI解耦为物理信息门控，仅RGB进入骨干网络，通过全局记忆库进行相似性驱动原型检索。该方法在保持分割性能的同时，有效分离了视觉外观学习与物理植被置信度，为多模态遥感数据融合提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对超分辨率遥感影像城市绿地提取中语义复用不足及NDVI与RGB特征混淆的问题，提出GMBFormer框架，将NDVI解耦为物理信息门控，仅RGB进入骨干网络，通过全局记忆库进行相似性驱动原型检索。该方法在保持分割性能的同时，有效分离了视觉外观学习与物理植被置信度，为多模态遥感数据融合提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: BMCR: Adaptive Backbone Module Composition via Reinforcement Learning for Remote Sensing Object Detection
quick_skim.abstract: In remote sensing object detection, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) excel at capturing local details while Vision Transformers (ViTs) are better at global context modeling. However, existing detectors typically rely on a single fixed backbone or a manually designed hybrid architecture, and thus fail to adaptively exploit these complementary strengths across inputs of diverse complexity. To address this limitation, we propose Backbone Module Composition via Reinforcement Learning (BMCR). BMCR dynamically assembles input-adaptive inference paths from reusable modules decomposed from off-the-shelf CNN and ViT backbones. To enable such cross-family composition, we first construct an extensible module toolbox. Specifically, we decompose representative CNN and ViT backbones into reusable functional modules and encapsulate each module with explicit structural, semantic, and computational metadata for compatibility-aware assembly. To bridge the gap between grid-based CNN features and token-based ViT representations, we design a lightweight Optimal Transport (OT) based transition interface that ensures distribution-aware alignment while respecting spatial consistency. The backbone composition process is then formulated as a sequential decision problem, in which a policy network progressively selects task-relevant modules according to intermediate multi-scale observations. To stabilize the joint optimization of reusable modules and the routing policy, we further develop an Adaptive Module Cooperative Optimization (AMCO) strategy that coordinates module updating, routing exploration, and reward assignment during training. On DOTA-v1.0, DOTA-v1.5 and DIOR-R, BMCR achieves 79.31\%, 73.41\% and 71.86\% mAP, respectively, surpassing strong static and dynamic baselines by up to 2.5 points while maintaining competitive efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: adaptive fusion of CNN and ViT backbones for remote sensing object detection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 自适应融合CNN和ViT骨干用于遥感目标检测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 自适应融合CNN和ViT骨干用于遥感目标检测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 自适应融合CNN和ViT骨干用于遥感目标检测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes BMCR, a reinforcement learning method to dynamically compose adaptive inference paths from CNN and ViT modules for remote sensing object detection, achieving multimodal feature fusion.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 遥感目标检测中CNN和ViT各有优势，但现有检测器依赖固定主干。本文提出BMCR，利用强化学习从CNN和ViT模块中动态组装自适应推理路径，实现跨家族特征融合。实验表明该方法在多个遥感检测数据集上优于单一主干和手动混合架构，为多模态特征融合提供了新范式。
